100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 28, 1926, the British Empire celebrated the arrival of a new era with the announcement of King Edward VII's plans to visit India, marking the first time a reigning monarch would travel to the subcontinent in over a century. Meanwhile, in the United States, debates over Prohibition intensified as the Senate discussed potential amendments to the Volstead Act, reflecting growing public discontent with the law. In Germany, economic instability continued to plague the Weimar Republic, with inflation rates soaring and political tension mounting.
Updated: Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 07:23:55 AM CDT
